meaning: adj.未改革的,未经宗教改革的
examples: The police are seen by many pundits as the last unreformed public service.
Then, a politically and unreformed and assertive China could be calling the shots in the most vibrant economic region of the world.
She saw the Polish words, dirty and all-knowing and unreformed, flinging mud on the clean English words until everything was equally dirty.
Nobody now expects his government to tackle one big, unreformed obstacle to business: employment-destroying labour laws.
If, on the other hand, the European parliament remains unreformed how truly democratic will the EU be?
